army_customisation.intro:
Army Customisation allows you to further individualise your force. Archetypes change how you build your army by moving around units in the Army Organisation Plan (AOP) or letting you take more of a unit than you would normally be allowed to. Legacies give access to armories and disciplines of legendary factions. Traits enhance your units with special abilities that they normally would not be able to have.

You may select up to the following:
0-1 Archetype, 0-1 Legacy, 0-2 Traits

If a Trait is taken, all models / units in the army must be upgraded with it, unless stated otherwise.

archetypes.name: Aspect Focus
archetypes.description:
- <Aspect> units can be taken as Troops.
- Non-<Aspect> Troops become Elite choices.

archetypes.name: Exemplars of the Shrines
archetypes.description: - All Exarchs must gain two Exarch powers and they are no longer unique.

archetypes.name: LIIVI
archetypes.description:
- The army got access to a single Vindicare assassin.
- One HQ selection must be a Farseer.

archetypes.name: Windhost
archetypes.description:
- Windriders can be taken as Troops.
- All units with less than 12" Movement must start the game as passengers inside a transport.
- Units that have no transport option and less than 12" Movement cannot be taken at all.

archetypes.name: Wraithhost
archetypes.description:
- Wraithblades and Wraithguard units can be taken as Troops.
- Non-<Wraith> Troops become Elite choices.

archetypes.name: Ynnari
archetypes.description:
- Ynnari become "Battle Brothers" for Dark Eldar.
- The army got access to the Ynnari Armory and the Revenant discipline.
- May not select any Legacy.

legacies.name: Fieldcraft
legacies.description: The army has access to equipment from the Alaitoc Craftworld in the Craftworld Armory.

legacies.name: Foresight of the Damned
legacies.description: The army has access to equipment from the Ulthwé Craftworld in the Craftworld Armory.

legacies.name: Stoic Endurance
legacies.description: The army has access to equipment from the Iyanden Craftworld in the Craftworld Armory.

legacies.name: Swordwind
legacies.description: The army has access to equipment from the Biel-Tan Craftworld in the Craftworld Armory.

legacies.name: Wild Host
legacies.description: The army has access to equipment from the Saim-Hann Craftworld in the Craftworld Armory.

traits.name: Children of Khaine
traits.description: The unit gains the "Deadly(6+)" ability for all melee weapons.

traits.name: Children of Morai-Heg
traits.description: If the unit is below starting strength, each model in it gains +1 attack.

traits.name: Children of Prophecy
traits.description: The unit may re-roll 1s and 2s for manifestating or denying psychic powers. Only for Psykers.

traits.name: Combined War Host
traits.description: The army must select a second Legacy. Each model may only select items from one Legacy armory.

traits.name: Elite Citizenry
traits.description: The unit may re-roll one to hit roll per activation.

traits.name: Expert Crafters
traits.description: The unit may re-roll one to wound or armor penetration roll per activation.

traits.name: Grim
traits.description: Every melee combat result is increased by +2 for the unit.

traits.name: Hail of Doom
traits.description: The "Shuriken" ability grants an additional -1 to the weapon's AP value.

traits.name: Masterful Shots
traits.description: The unit gains the "Sunder(1)" ability for all ranged attacks.

traits.name: Masters of Concealment
traits.description: The unit gains the benefit of light cover until its first activation.

traits.name: Mobile Fighters
traits.description: If the unit disembarked from a transport this round, all of its weapons gain the "Decimate" ability for this activation.

traits.name: Savage Blades
traits.description: If the unit uses a Charge order or gets charged by an enemy unit, all of its melee weapons gain -1 AP for this activation.

traits.name: Students of Vaul
traits.description: The unit ignores temporary vehicle damage on a roll of 4+.
